name: ds2-adam-source-continuation-v1
frozen_at: Base execution design frozen 2026-07-15 before reading the completed ds2 Adam-source-factorial aggregate; natural-stratum analysis amendment frozen 2026-07-16 after source selection but before loading a model or observing any continuation outcome

protocol_amendment.reason: The base freeze specified probes and outcomes but did not uniquely define the longitudinal natural-stratum estimand or persistence classification.
protocol_amendment.timing: Added after the factorial selected update-32 M, but before continuation namespace creation, model load, or outcome observation.
protocol_amendment.scope: Defines symmetric M-donor contrasts, paired longitudinal inference, hard h0/h1 guards, and selection-conditional claim language. It does not change the selected source, checkpoint, arms, horizon, probes, training data, or evaluation bank.

question: Does an optimizer/data source nominated by the frozen one-step factorial exert persistent causal influence during the next 32 natural-scale AdamW updates?

scope.claim: This is a donor-route persistence and dynamics experiment. It is not a test that wolf is globally canonical, and it does not by itself identify when an alternative route becomes stronger than a wolf route.
scope.outcome_blindness: The aggregate file may be parsed, but the continuation runner may access or use only its preregistered continuation_decision and provenance. Effect estimates, gates, and per-seed measurements must never be indexed, accessed, or used by the runner.
scope.snapshot_status: Source payloads are named analysis snapshots with exact_resume_claim=false. Continuation is a controlled reconstruction whose learning-rate schedule and identity traces must be proved against Stage A.
scope.transplant_semantics: Selected M/V donors are patched once at the source checkpoint, then branch-local AdamW moments evolve normally for 32 updates. They are never repatched.
scope.natural_stratum: Always cross theta. Cross every nominated external axis among M,V,D independently. Inactive M/V are native to theta and inactive D matches theta. This follows the qualified contrast into natural strata rather than preserving its one-step marginal averaging.

selection.axis_union_resolver: Take the union of M,V,D letters appearing in selected_effects. Cross those axes independently with theta. Presence of T adds no external axis.

execution.identity_definition: T=M=V=D; exactly PPPP and CCCC per seed
execution.batch_indices: For branch offset h=1..32 use the exact global next_indices(source_update+h-1) under the frozen seed order; D changes only the paired P/C dataset bank.

evaluation.claim_boundary: Probe trajectories measure donor-route persistence and branch dynamics. They do not identify an identical-state wolf-vs-nonwolf marginal-utility crossover.

frozen_analysis.design: For the selected M-only branch, V and D remain native/matching to theta. This is a longitudinal 2x2 theta-by-M natural-stratum design, not a continuation of the complete 2^4 marginal factorial estimand.
frozen_analysis.primary_m_effect: Delta_M = (delta_preference_theta + delta_control_theta) / 2. This is the preference-coded first-moment donor effect in the two natural co-adapted strata.
frozen_analysis.native_match_advantage: 0.5 * ((U(T=P,M=P)-U(T=P,M=C)) + (U(T=C,M=C)-U(T=C,M=P))); secondary co-adaptation contrast and never called the M main effect.

behavior.change_from_h0: For every arm and unit first subtract that arm's h0 value, then form delta_preference_theta, delta_control_theta, Delta_M, and H. This per-unit longitudinal difference-in-differences is the reported trajectory estimand.

numeric_loss.utility_definition: U = -NLL, so positive contrasts mean lower NLL
numeric_loss.endpoint_and_auc: For each reported NLL utility contrast, report h32 and per-row trapezoidal AUC/32 using the same frozen offsets; these are secondary and do not determine the persistence classification.
numeric_loss.change_from_h0: Subtract each arm's per-row h0 utility before forming every theta stratum, Delta_M, endpoint, or AUC contrast, exactly parallel to the behavior longitudinal difference-in-differences.

trajectory.natural_stratum_entry: behavior wolf-margin Delta_M at h=1; this establishes whether the selected marginal-factorial M route is already present in the natural V=D=theta strata
trajectory.primary_auc: prompt-level trapezoidal integral of behavior wolf-margin Delta_M over the frozen offsets divided by 32

inference.interval: paired percentile 95% CI within each training seed
inference.longitudinal_pairing: Use the same resampled prompt indices across all four arms and every offset; independently use the same row indices across all arms, offsets, and both numeric banks.
inference.training_loss_inference: Report exact theta-stratified and cumulative training-loss curves descriptively; do not treat updates or microbatches as iid bootstrap units.

selection_conditional_classification.entry_positive: h1 behavior-margin Delta_M paired-bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 0 in both seeds
selection_conditional_classification.endpoint_positive: h32 behavior-margin Delta_M paired-bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 0 in both seeds
selection_conditional_classification.auc_positive: AUC/32 behavior-margin Delta_M paired-bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 0 in both seeds
selection_conditional_classification.joint_test: The single persistence claim is a conjunctive intersection-union criterion requiring all three positive gates in both seeds; no multiplicity correction is needed for that joint claim. Individual trajectory points, NLL contrasts, and marginal criteria remain descriptive nominal intervals.
selection_conditional_classification.later_positive_entry_unresolved: at least one of endpoint_positive or auc_positive, but not entry_positive; report later donor-induced dynamics with unresolved natural-stratum entry, not persistence
selection_conditional_classification.entry_positive_later_unresolved: entry_positive but not both endpoint_positive and auc_positive; report an established entry route with unresolved persistence, not transience or disappearance
selection_conditional_classification.no_complete_replicated_pattern: none of the patterns above; report which criteria pass and do not accept a null effect from an interval crossing zero
selection_conditional_classification.claim_boundary: This classification is conditional on selecting M at update 32 with the same two seeds and 30 prompts. Its nominal intervals are post-selection and are not an independent confirmation or population-level seed inference.

hard_guards.h0: Within each theta and seed, the two M-source variants must have exactly identical per-prompt behavior and per-row NLL arrays.
hard_guards.h1: Every natural-stratum arm must reproduce its exact Stage-B candidate's numeric loss, gradient norm, and reconstructed per-prompt/per-row post-step evaluation arrays within frozen tolerances.
hard_guards.h1_unit_contrast: Continuation h1 per-prompt and per-row M contrasts must reproduce the corresponding differences between Stage-B candidate change vectors within frozen tolerances.
hard_guards.identity: The PPPP and CCCC arms must reproduce all 32 Stage-A scalar updates and the exact update-64 LoRA, exp_avg, and exp_avg_sq semantic hashes.
hard_guards.failure_policy: Any failed hard guard invalidates inference until diagnosed and repaired.

frozen_analysis.multiplicity: No familywise correction. Report all frozen trajectory points and label nominal prompt/row intervals as selection-conditional. Do not select an onset checkpoint or make significance claims from individual trajectory points.

artifacts.completion_policy: cell.json is the only reusable arm sentinel; interrupted numbered attempts remain preserved
